title: South Korea Just Nominated Its First Female Prime Minister in 20 Years
script: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung nominated Han Seong-sook as prime minister this week — if Parliament confirms her, she becomes South Korea's first female prime minister in 20 years. Han, 69, spent years as CEO of Naver, South Korea's largest internet company, before entering politics. The last woman to hold the post was Han Myeong-sook in 2006. Parliament still needs to vote. The nomination itself is already historic.
